rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in the references; the editorial discusses it multiple times as a key contribution in the special issue. The authors summarize its core research role (goal motivation and continuation decisions in MOOCs), its substantive insight (midpoint abandonment hazard/inverted U engagement), and use it to illustrate how online education research advances marketing theory. This gives the citation a clear functional role in the citing paper's framing and synthesis of the issue.
relation_description: The editorial uses Lu, Bradlow, and Hutchinson (2022) as a central example of special-issue work on online learning, summarizing its model-based findings to support the issue's narrative about MOOC engagement and educational delivery. The cited study is integrated into the editorial's conceptual positioning of how marketing frameworks can explain education consumption dynamics.
